subproblems.statement: Does 2024-2026 LLM agent / tool-use literature already formalize a cascade rule where each tool call's output narrows the downstream tool-selection distribution?
Is the cuneiform Behistun-cascade (monolingual → bilingual → trilingual lock-in) functionally equivalent to ReAct-style chain-of-thought or to multi-agent debate convergence?
Is there a measurable LLM benchmark improvement from explicit bilingual cross-check between two tools versus single-tool reasoning?
